assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response mentions TPU slices will be preempted and recreated but doesn't explicitly explain the atomic recreation process or contrast it with rolling upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response doesn't explain that maintenance on any single slice affects all slices in the multislice environment

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response clearly states that the training process will be interrupted and terminated, and mentions pods will restart

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention potential resource availability issues or blocking behavior that could cause extended downtime

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response recommends increasing checkpoint frequency leading up to maintenance and considering manually pausing training if maintenance is imminent

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response includes configuration examples for automatic checkpointing and mentions ensuring training scripts can resume from arbitrary checkpoints

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention maintenance exclusions or the option to defer maintenance events

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: The response doesn't discuss maintenance windows or scheduling maintenance during planned breaks
